title: The government of Prime Minister Takaichi faces opposition resistance and parliamentary deadlock
summary: The ruling coalition promotes reforms such as the reduction of seats and the secondary city law, while the opposition criticizes the lack of dialogue and demands the normalization of Congress.

title: Torrential rains cause landslides and floods in Kyushu and western Japan
summary: The authorities issue alerts for linear rain bands and risk of disasters in several prefectures, with evacuations and material damage.

title: Global markets react cautiously to Fed comments and mixed economic data
summary: The statements of the Federal Reserve chairman on inflation and the slowdown in manufacturing ISM impact currencies, bonds, and stock markets.

title: Seitaro Imato, living national treasure of shamisen, dies at 90
summary: The master of nagauta, recognized for his mastery of the shamisen and his work in preserving traditional music, leaves an invaluable artistic legacy.

title: Japan national team players express frustration after World Cup elimination
summary: Tanaka and Doan self-critically analyze their performance against Brazil and commit to working for the next World Cup cycle.

title: LINE Yahoo makes acquisition offer for Kakaku.com higher than those of European funds
summary: The Japanese company formalizes a binding proposal to buy the review portal at a price 17% higher than that of its competitors.

title: Victor Willis, singer of "Y.M.C.A.", dies at 74
summary: The iconic vocalist of Village People, famous for 70s disco hits, leaves a musical legacy that marked several generations.

title: Sony will end production of disc games for PlayStation starting in 2025
summary: The company announces that upcoming major titles will only be available via digital download, following the market trend.

title: Japan Football Association asks for Hajime Moriyasu's continuity as national team coach
summary: After the World Cup, the JFA offers a new one-year contract to the national football team coach.

title: Fukuda Kootaro, 20, wins the Honinbo title in Go
summary: The young prodigy defeats Ichiriki Ryo and becomes the new champion of the most prestigious Go tournament in Japan.

title: Fujii Sota achieves his seventh consecutive Kisei title in shogi
summary: The shogi prodigy extends his dominance by defeating Hattori Shinichiro 3-0 in the tournament final.

title: A 10-year-old boy dies after disappearing during class and being found in a waterfall
summary: The minor, with special educational needs, left the classroom to go to the bathroom and was found dead 1.5 km away.

title: Magnitude 6.0 earthquake shakes Iwate coast without causing tsunami
summary: The earthquake, with epicenter in the Pacific Ocean off Iwate, reached seismic intensity 4 in several cities without triggering a tsunami warning.

title: Two and a half years later, recovery from the Noto earthquake advances with interregional support
summary: 99 municipalities send personnel to help with reconstruction while local female divers resume seaweed fishing as a symbol of normalization.

title: Chubu Electric manipulated seismic data at the Hamaoka nuclear power plant
summary: The company excluded records of waves that exceeded safety parameters, pointing to a possible cover-up of irregularities.

title: Father attacks his son and is arrested; bodies of his ex-wife and daughter found in the house
summary: The man fled after the attack but was arrested for attempted murder, while authorities investigate the deaths of the other victims.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi travels to India to reinforce strategic and economic cooperation
summary: On her first official visit, she seeks to expand security cooperation and counter China's influence in the region.

title: Japan reforms the Japanese language teacher exam to facilitate qualification of foreigners
summary: The government plans to increase the frequency of the national exam to several times a year to respond to the demand for resident workers.

title: Tokyo Gas qualifies for the national amateur baseball tournament for the sixth consecutive year
summary: The team will represent Tokyo in the traditional city tournament after prevailing in the qualifying stage with a solid performance.

title: Earthquake in Venezuela causes nearly 2,000 deaths and destroys thousands of buildings
summary: The earthquake, one of the deadliest in recent history, leaves a trail of devastation and a humanitarian crisis in the South American country.
